# @mongez/concat-route

> A tiny, dependency-free path joiner. Concatenates any number of path segments into a single normalized leading-slash path. Handles stray slashes, falsy segments, and embedded `//`. One default export.

This package has a single public surface: the default export `concatRoute(...segments: string[]): string`. Nothing else ships from the entry point.

## Docs

- [Overview](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/hassanzohdy/mongez-concat-route/main/skills/overview.md): Pitch, install, mental model.
- [README](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/hassanzohdy/mongez-concat-route/main/README.md): Marketing-style index with full edge-case table.
- [Changelog](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/hassanzohdy/mongez-concat-route/main/CHANGELOG.md): Release notes.

## Reference

- [concat-route](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/hassanzohdy/mongez-concat-route/main/skills/concat-route.md): The full function reference — signature, normalization rules, behavior table, edge cases, return contract.

## Recipes

- [Recipes](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/hassanzohdy/mongez-concat-route/main/skills/recipes.md): Building API URLs, locale prefixes, base paths, paginated routes, sub-resources.

## Quick rules

1. The function takes any number of string segments and returns one normalized path.
2. The result always begins with exactly one `/`. The only time it ends with `/` is the empty result `"/"`.
3. Falsy segments (`""`, `null`, `undefined`, `0`, `false`) are dropped silently.
4. Leading and trailing slashes on each segment are stripped before joining.
5. Runs of `/` anywhere in the joined result collapse to a single `/`.
6. The function is segment-only — it does NOT handle absolute URLs, query strings, or hash fragments specially. Pass a `?query` and it will be prefixed with a `/`. For full URLs, use the platform `URL` object.

## Optional

- [Full single-file reference (llms-full.txt)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/hassanzohdy/mongez-concat-route/main/llms-full.txt): All reference content concatenated.
